item.title: Version 0.12.4
item.description:
What changed

  • cut.sh: 1 fix from /ultra round 6 (notes cleanup on resume)
  • cut.sh: 4 fixes from /ultra round 5 (OpenAI's late catches)
  • cut.sh + runbook: 5 fixes from /ultra round 4
  • cut.sh + runbook: 6 fixes from /ultra round 3
  • cut.sh + runbook: 7 fixes from /ultra round 2
  • cut.sh + runbook: 11 fixes from /ultra round 1
  • cut.sh: 6 fixes from rival review
  • Add scripts/cut.sh + Mac mini build host runbook
  • fix(hook+clipboard): accurate log messages + scope sleep-safe stale-lock
  • fix(hook): guard pgrep check after wait loop before launching new Kera
  • fix(hook+clipboard): fix lock-steal race regression, add 30-min stale timeout, trim whitespace
  • fix(hook): fix 0-byte pid race, add pgrep wait loop before relaunch
  • fix(hook+clipboard): fix POSIX $$ PID bug, harden mkdir failure, return setString result
  • fix(hook): fix mktemp suffix bug, add mkdir-p and stale-lock recovery; guard empty transcript
  • docs(hook): update README to match mktemp log naming and lockdir behavior
  • fix(hook+clipboard): harden post-merge hook and extract shared transcript clipboard helper
  • feat(menu-bar): add Copy Transcript to right-click context menu
  • hooks: track post-merge in .githooks/ + fully detach background build
  • fix(cancel): update meta.backend when retranscription cancel arrives post-commit
  • fix(cancel): three more from ultra round 4
  • fix(cancel): three more edge cases from ultra round 3
  • fix(cancel): harden four edge cases found in rival ultra review
  • fix(cancel): remove eager cancelAll cleanup race + guard retranscribing cancel path
  • fix(cancel): restore watchdog-benign CancellationError path; track currentJob
  • fix(cancel): set isProcessing=true synchronously; guard completed sessions
  • fix(cancel): prevent zombie resume + harden cancellation error handling
  • test(cancel): add TranscriptionCancellationTests (9 tests A–I)
  • feat(cancel): wire Cancel button through MenuBar UI + AppDelegate
  • feat(cancel): add cancelAll() to TranscriptionWorker + Soniox cleanup
  • fix(reprocess-popover): Mode picker no longer dismisses the panel
  • fix(orphan-salvage): refuse to silently delete crash-truncated WAVs
  • fix(mic-capture): pass format: nil to rebuild installTap (stop SIGABRT)
  • fix(detector): explicit Optional patterns for older-Swift CI compat
  • ci(tests): add SwiftPM build+test workflow on push/PR
  • fix(keychain): refuse empty writes + log every set/delete; ROADMAP note
  • feat(library): surface failed AI summaries in row (⚠ indicator)
  • polish(display): tighten humanFallbackTitle parser + format
  • fix(display): show human-readable date title for untitled sessions
  • fix(ui): make Reprocess… trigger button hit area cover full chrome
  • fix(ui): make Run button hit area cover full chrome in Reprocess popover
  • fix(about): rival round-1 — waveform fallback + accessibilityHidden
  • feat(about): show real app icon instead of SF Symbol placeholder
  • chore(handoff-dmg): track package manifests; gitignore node_modules
  • docs(architecture): note planned SQLite cache layer beside flat files
  • fix(keychain): test-isolation seam — tests can't touch real keychain anymore
  • chore: bump version to 0.12.1
  • fix(speaker-editor): preserve ai_title, drop stale names, harden auto-detect
  • fix(speaker-editor): auto-composed name extends with distinct second rename
  • test(speaker-editor): cover meeting_name compose-from-scratch (A–H)
  • fix(speaker-editor): compose meeting_name on edit when previously empty
  • docs(readme): centered hero with logo, point downloads to kera-releases
  • docs(ux-audit): fix probe.md path ref → __WORKSPACE/yonios-tools/docs/probe.md
  • fix(summary): guard ai_summary rewrite against sentinel labels from remove/markAsNonSpeech
  • fix(summary): propagate speaker renames into ai_summary on rewriteTranscriptAndMeta
  • docs: ROADMAP — v0.12 entry + park source-separation idea
  • chore: bump version to 0.12
  • feat(whisperkit): plumb word-level timings through to BleedFilter
  • docs(backfill): sync spec to landed implementation (rev 8)
  • fix(backfill): /ultra round 7 — exhaustive provider switch + clear filter before View
  • fix(backfill): /ultra round 6 — clarify .clean(count:0) edge case in doc
  • fix(backfill): /ultra round 5 — sync stale doc comments to .partial behavior
  • fix(backfill): /ultra round 4 — correct footer button copy
  • fix(backfill): /ultra round 3 — cancel pre-.generating, partial for all-empty, inFlight seeding
  • fix(backfill): /ultra round 2 — cancellation guards + consistent progress totals + retry disable
  • fix(backfill): /ultra round 1 — 6 correctness fixes
  • fix(backfill): polish copy gaps — banner/modal/chip/toast/footer text
  • feat(backfill): Commit 4 — Library footer/toast + failed filter + docs
  • feat(backfill): Commit 3 — KeraConfirmModal + Settings banner/chip + injection
  • feat(backfill): Commit 2 — BulkSummaryRunner + coordinator wiring + quit handling
  • feat(backfill): Commit 1 — model layer: types + cost estimator + coordinator skeleton + settings
  • fix(popover): animate footer strip in/out when modelStatus or pendingTranscriptions changes
  • fix(transcription): clear heartbeat modelStatus after Me/Them local pipeline
  • fix(handoff-dmg): clearer errors for version fallback and icon positioning
  • fix(handoff-dmg): assert CDN link was actually patched out
  • fix(handoff-dmg): await document.fonts.ready before screenshot
  • fix(handoff-dmg): embed IBM Plex fonts locally, remove CDN dependency
  • fix(handoff-dmg): decouple @2x requirement, remove dead README icon ref
  • fix(handoff-dmg): harden SSH check, document CDN font risk, clean README
  • fix(handoff-dmg): use puppeteer-core correctly in export.sh
  • feat: wire handoff-dmg background into DMG build pipeline
  • docs: backfill flow stub plan + designer handoff
  • docs: ROADMAP — detectLanguage fix on main, pending next release
  • fix(whisperkit): detectLanguage returns ("he", 0) on all-Hebrew audio
  • docs: ROADMAP — v0.11 entry (AI Naming sidebar fix on top of v0.10.5)
  • chore: bump version to 0.11
  • docs: ROADMAP — queue 8b93db8 as v0.10.5.1 hotfix candidate
  • docs: ROADMAP — v0.10.5 entry (Local Me/Them + BleedFilter rewrite)
  • fix(settings): fix sidebar label truncation by replacing Spacer with frame(maxWidth:.infinity)
  • chore: bump version to 0.10.5
  • tests: standalone replay of local Me/Them on real recordings
  • Local Me/Them: dispatch helper extraction + uniqueness tests
  • TranscriptionWorker: WhisperKit-backed Me/Them pipeline
  • ReprocessRegistry: add (.local, .meAndThem) as a supported variant
  • BleedFilter: word-overlap bleed detection (replaces temporal overlap)
  • docs: local-me-them plan (rev 4, rival-reviewed)
  • fix(settings): top-align icon+badge and cap sidebar label at 2 lines
  • fix(settings): prevent ampersand widow in sidebar labels
  • chore: retire yonibloch/mini-granola-releases — drop from authorization list
  • chore: update repo URL after GitHub rename mini-granola-swift → kera
  • chore: post-rebrand cleanup — remove residual Mini-Granola references
  • docs: archive v0.10.4 window-chrome-system plan + rival history
  • feat(settings): KeraSegmented for all 4 pickers + kerInk tint propagation; bump 0.10.4.2
  • fix(settings): language detection defaults to ON for new installs
  • chore: bump version to 0.10.4.1 (transcript-window hotfix)
  • fix(reader): actually clamp transcript window — disable NSHostingView size propagation
  • docs: ROADMAP + README — v0.10.4 window chrome system
  • init bug log
  • chore: bump version to 0.10.4
  • fix(window-chrome): commit 5 — /rival review fixes (4 bugs + 1 polish)
  • feat(window-chrome): commit 4 — hairline normalization (Divider audit + Bucket B)
  • feat(window-chrome): commit 3 — KeraSegmented + system-color sweep
  • feat(window-chrome): commit 2 — KeraRowSelectionStyle + selection wash audit
  • feat(window-chrome): commit 1 — applyKeraChrome + KeraTitle + KeraDivider + flip 6 windows
  • fix(settings): rename "Auto-name with AI" to reflect summary gating
  • fix(reader): clamp transcript window to screen + fix cascade offset
  • feat(library-ai-summaries): commit 4 — UI: SummaryLine + SummarySection + search + docs
  • feat(library-ai-summaries): commit 3 — worker hook + in-flight tracking
  • feat(library-ai-summaries): commit 2 — LLMSummaryGenerator + transcript loader
  • feat(library-ai-summaries): commit 1 — model layer (additive Codable)
  • fix(menubar): right-click menu uses the new structured Reprocess flow
  • chore: post-rebrand sweep — drop Mini-Granola leftovers, ready for sharing
  • chore(theme): sweep raw SwiftUI colors → Kera tokens
  • fix(reprocess): make cache state visible per-mode in the popover
  • feat(reprocess): step 6 — ReprocessPopover UI behind KeraReprocessPopoverEnabled
  • feat(reprocess): step 5 — worker.activateArtifact (11-step Switch lifecycle)
  • feat(reprocess): step 4 — worker.cacheCurrentVariant + ReprocessArtifact
  • feat(reprocess): step 3 — ReprocessRegistry (pure types + per-session gating)
  • fix(kera): migration loop + reprocess-cache prep (Codable + failSession restore)
  • feat(kera): drop in designer's hand-traced spiral assets (K-003 receipt)
  • feat(kera): dark mode — Editorial Night palette + appearance resolver
  • chore(kera): migrate inverted-token foregrounds to ker tokens
  • feat(kera): icons — spiral menubar glyph + Kera AppIcon.icns
  • feat(kera): commit 8/8 — point Sparkle at yonibloch/kera-releases
  • feat(kera): commit 7/8 — rename Mini-Granola → Kera (bundle, executable, paths, data migration)
  • feat(kera): commit 6/8 — drop dead mg* tokens and dynamic() helper
  • feat(kera): commit 5/8 — sweep every UI surface onto Kera tokens
  • feat(kera): commit 4/8 — menubar popover redesigned to Plate 06
  • feat(kera): commit 3/8 — Kera color tokens (paper, ink, ink-red)
  • feat(kera): commit 2/8 — bundle IBM Plex fonts + Kera font helpers
  • feat(kera): commit 1/8 — force light appearance, remove dark mode
  • fix(ux): UX cleanup round 2 — polish + discoverability
  • fix(ux): v0.9.52 — UX cleanup round 1 (probe-audit findings)
  • feat(dev): v0.9.51 — Dev mode toggle for UI automation
  • chore(release): v0.9.50 — custom vocabulary
  • feat(vocab): custom vocabulary field threaded to Soniox, WhisperKit, LLM
  • docs: architecture map (module graph + runtime flow diagrams)
  • docs: project CLAUDE.md with release workflow + main-push authorization
  • docs(roadmap): v0.9.47 → v0.9.49 shipped
  • feat(storage): auto-salvage orphan sessions with Process/Ignore/Remove
  • fix(notifications): mode-aware HUD + notification body copy
  • fix(library): restore pill chrome on Re-process menu button
  • feat(ui): No diarization chrome + checkmark re-process menu
  • feat(transcription): no-diar early-exit in full-diar branch
  • feat(transcription): add .noDiarization mode + settings radio
  • fix(storage): surface decode errors + systemAudioActive optional
  • fix(reader): suppress Me/Them offer on mic-only sessions
  • feat(reader): word-sync upgrade banner for local sessions via Soniox
  • fix(me-them): gate Re-process as Me/Them on dual-stream sessions only
  • docs(roadmap): v0.9.34 → v0.9.46 shipped + Me/Them banner TODO
  • fix(me-them): actually flip re-process button into pending state on click
  • fix(me-them): in-flight button state + idempotent retranscribe + no-unknowns in Me/Them
  • feat(me-them): auto-upgrade local→Soniox when re-processing as Me/Them
  • fix(me-them): refuse Me/Them on local sessions instead of breaking them
  • feat(library): mode-flip button + right-click menu on Meeting Library rows
  • chore: bump version to 0.9.46
  • feat(transcription): Me/Them pipeline + 1:1 guard + finalize notification
  • feat(ui): Me+N header + [Me/Them] pill + offer banner + retranscribe skeleton + 1:1 footer + recording chrome
  • feat(ui-guards): block Speaker Editor entry points for Me/Them sessions
  • feat(meta): SessionMetadata Me/Them fields + error persistence + job mode fields
  • feat(settings): TranscriptionMode enum + outcome-led radio + don't-ask-again
  • fix(calendar): filter declined/tentative + displayName fallback for dial-ins
  • fix(bleed-filter): preserve words + shift system WordTiming by syncOffsetMs
  • refactor(session-store): SessionMetaUpdate struct + explicit-clear semantics
  • fix(speaker-editor): play sample from best turn, not firstAtSec (v0.9.45)
  • fix(transcript): align timestamps with audio end-to-end (v0.9.44)
  • fix(retranscribe): preserve original diarization backend on re-process
  • feat(matcher): Stage A' + Stage A from speaker-id handoff
  • fix(library): collapse durable-isMe profile name into "Me" in participant lists
  • feat(voice-store): enroll Me (Stages 0+1a+1b)
  • docs: open project "cracking diarization at scale"
  • feat(library): per-session confirm-and-dismiss for attribution warnings
  • feat(library): tier 2 (per-session) + tier 3 (cross-session) bulk retag
  • feat(library): per-row reassignment context menu (Path B tier 1)
  • feat(library): attribution-confidence badging per sample row (Path A)
  • fix(library): full-row tap target in speaker list
  • feat(library): filter sub-1.5s utterances + cap at top-50 newest
  • chore(release): v0.9.34
  • feat(speakers-library): multi-utterance samples + real waveforms + lazy hydration
  • feat(theme): stableAvatarColor honors colorSeed + cross-surface threading
  • feat(voice-profile-store): colorSeed + durable isMe + threading
  • feat(audio): AudioUtils.computePeaks + bestAudio helper
  • docs(roadmap): v0.9.30 → v0.9.33 shipped
  • fix(sparkle): implement gentle-reminders so LSUIElement users see updates
  • feat(reader): banner to upgrade pre-v0.9.31 Soniox sessions to word-sync
  • chore(release): v0.9.31 + session retranscribe utility
  • feat(transcript): preserve per-token timestamps for exact word-level sync
  • feat(settings): make diagnostics-export privacy story explicit
  • feat(settings): export anonymized match diagnostics
  • feat(speaker-editor): Me/user-real-name consolidation banner for existing sessions
  • feat(speakers-library): ghost rows for orphan transcript names
  • feat(voice-profile-store): log top-3 similarity candidates per cluster for tuning data
  • fix(speaker-editor): v0.9.28 — sample-trust + icon re-render
  • feat(speaker-editor): v0.9.27 — inline tag row + recency-sorted chips + session skip state + new dock icon
  • docs(roadmap): park folder-slug rename; promote B + C to Parked with full specs
  • docs(roadmap): v0.9.26 shipped — A2 cross-session mutations + version bump
  • feat(speakers-library): v0.9.25-A2 sub 2c — cross-session merge UI
  • feat(speakers-library): v0.9.25-A2 sub 2b — bulk mutation UI + 7s undo toast + always-confirm alert
  • feat(speaker-service): v0.9.25-A2 sub 2a — decouple phase 2/3 + cross-session orchestrator + APFS-backed undo store
  • docs(roadmap): move v0.9.25-A1 to Shipped + trim in-progress section
  • feat(speakers-library): v0.9.25-A1 — standalone Speakers Library window
  • fix(speaker-editor): sync window title + header on rename (v0.9.24.2)
  • fix(reader): update NSWindow titlebar when meta.meeting_name changes (v0.9.24.1)
  • docs: v0.9.24 ship — Info.plist bump + ROADMAP Shipped + README Speaker Editor
  • fix(speaker-editor): propagate renames into meta.json meeting_name
  • feat(speaker-editor): phase 4 — non-speech visibility in reader + footer undo hint
  • feat(app): window activation coordinator — Dock icon + Cmd-Tab on demand
  • fix(speaker-editor): three bugs caught during phase 3 QA
  • feat(speaker-editor): phase 3 — focus mode + standalone editor window
  • docs(roadmap): move Speaker Editor from Parked → In progress (v0.9.24 WIP)
  • feat(speaker-editor): phase 2 UI — editor sheet, rename/merge popovers, toast/undo
  • feat(speaker-editor): phase 1 backend + v0.9.23 avatar pass
  • feat(meeting-library): new Meeting Library window + warm palette unification (v0.9.22)
  • docs: refresh README for v0.9.21 + add ROADMAP.md
  • feat(transcript-reader): Copy button + Summarize placeholder (v0.9.21)
  • fix(transcript-reader): ⌘W closes the window (v0.9.20)
  • feat(detector): auto-detect Google Meet and Microsoft Teams web (v0.9.19)
  • fix(settings): clarify the auto-record toggle
  • docs: refresh README for v0.9.16
  • feat: Mini Granola UI redesign — HUD, Settings, Tagging, Transcript reader, light mode (v0.9.16)
  • fix(AppDelegate): async-safe scan-trigger locking
  • chore: remove dead NSMenu helpers and ignore .claude/

item.title: Version 0.12.3 — Stop the orphan-salvage scanner from deleting crashed recordings
item.description:
Critical data-loss hotfix on top of 0.12.2

The orphan-session scanner that runs at every launch — the mechanism designed to recover recordings from crashes — was itself deleting crashed recordings. The trigger is exact: a SIGABRT crash leaves mic.wav with an unfinalized RIFF/data-chunk-size header (Swift's dealloc never runs to patch them). On next launch, OrphanSessionSalvage called AVAudioFile(forReading:), got length=0 back, the if dur < 5 auto-remove guard fired, and the entire session dir was silently removeItem'd.

If 0.12.2 fixed the crash you were seeing on Zoom disconnect — but you launched 0.12.2 with a crashed recording from 0.12.1 still on disk — that recording was destroyed by 0.12.2's launch sequence. This release fixes that.

Two independent guards now sit between the scanner and your data:

  • Duration estimate falls back to file size. All Kera mic recordings are 16 kHz mono Int16 = 32,000 bytes/sec. When AVAudioFile can't read the unfinalized header, the byte-count estimate kicks in and yields a real duration. A 56-min recording reports as ~3358s instead of 0s.
  • Hard size guard on the auto-remove path. The scanner refuses to delete any file larger than ~200 KB regardless of computed duration. If the duration math ever regresses again, the size gate alone keeps the data on disk so the orphan banner can surface it.

Two regression tests pin the new behavior — one with the exact SIGABRT-truncated WAV shape (RIFF size 0, data chunk size 0), one with a malformed-header large file as belt-and-braces.

If you've already lost a recording to this bug, this release does not bring it back. The file was unlinked, not Trashed, and won't survive without an external backup. Sorry.

item.title: Version 0.12.2 — Mic capture crash hotfix (Zoom disconnect)
item.description:
Hotfix: app no longer aborts when an audio session ends mid-recording

If you were recording while a Zoom call (or any process holding the audio device — Meet, FaceTime, AirPods reconnect, USB mic unplug) released its grip, Kera could die with SIGABRT and lose the in-flight recording. AVAudioEngine was throwing an Objective-C NSException from installTap during the route-flux rebuild, and Swift can't catch ObjC exceptions — so the throw became a hard process abort.

The rebuild path now passes format: nil to installTap, which lets AVAudioEngine introspect the bus state itself at install time and pick a self-consistent format. By construction, the format-mismatch race that caused the crash can't fire on this path. Apple-documented safe form for the API.

If you have a recording that was lost to this crash on 0.12.1, the existing v0.9.49 orphan-session banner picks it up automatically on next launch — click Process to recover the audio that survived (mic.wav writes incrementally to disk; SIGABRT doesn't roll those writes back).

Behind the scenes

  • Test isolation for the keychain. swift test can no longer write to your real login keychain — a structural seam (in-memory provider auto-selected when XCTest is loaded) plus a fail-closed guard on every keychain-touching test. Closes the historical bug where the backfill test suite was overwriting the user's real Anthropic API key on every test run.
  • Defensive empty-write guard. KeychainHelper.set with an empty value now logs a notice and refuses to write — protects against any future code path that could silently blank an API key. Use delete(service:) to intentionally clear.
  • CI on every push. A GitHub Actions workflow now runs swift test on every push to main and every PR. 476 tests, ~30-60s per run with cache.

The deeper hardening of the audio path — small ObjC @try/@catch bridge so any residual AVFoundation exception funnels into the existing markBroken() path instead of aborting, plus tighter format pre-validation — is planned for a follow-up release. This hotfix addresses the known crash trigger; the bridge is belt-and-braces against unknown future variants.

item.title: Version 0.12.1 — Speaker Editor rename now names the meeting
item.description:
If a recording finalized without a meeting name (calendar lookup missed, AI title generator failed, all remote speakers collapsed to the fallback), the Speaker Editor previously couldn't repair it. Labeling SPEAKER_01 as "Uri Vardi" updated the transcript but left the meeting nameless and the folder slug raw.

This release closes that gap. When you label an unknown speaker:

  • Empty meeting_name → composed from scratch. "Call with Uri Vardi", or "Call with Uri Vardi (Sprint Planning)" if a calendar title exists, or "Call with Uri Vardi (My AI Title)" when the LLM-generated title was stored in ai_title.
  • Distinct second rename extends the name. Label SPEAKER_01 → Alice, then SPEAKER_02 → Bob → "Call with Alice, Bob". The second label was silently no-op'ing before.
  • Stale auto-names are cleared. Mark the last remote speaker as non-speech with no calendar/AI title → meeting goes back to nameless rather than freezing on the now-incorrect "Call with Alice".
  • AI-generated parentheticals survive renames. Previously a rename silently dropped the (My AI Title) suffix.
  • Manual free-text titles are protected. "Call with my dentist" is preserved by the strict pre-edit canonical match — only auto-composed names get rewritten.

Folder slug on disk still doesn't auto-update — that needs a system-wide URL invalidation notification (deferred to a future release). 14 new tests, 465 total.

item.title: Version 0.12 — Backfill flow + word-level highlighting on local + Hebrew detect fix
item.description:
Backfill Flow — bulk-summarize past meetings

Enable AI Naming with a backlog of un-summarized meetings? You'll see a banner in Settings → AI Naming offering to "Summarize all" past meetings. The flow:

  • Banner in Settings → AI Naming when un-summarized count > 0. "Not now" dismisses; resurfaces when count grows by 10+ past dismissal.
  • Confirm modal with a live cost estimate (tokens × your provider's pricing). Disabled until the estimate loads.
  • Ambient progress — chip in Settings + footer strip in Library while the bulk runner walks the un-summarized set, 4-wide concurrent.
  • End-of-batch toast: "All caught up." (clean, auto-dismiss 6s) or "Retry failed / Show failed" (partial, manual dismiss). "Show failed" activates a Library filter.

Word-level highlighting on the local engine

WhisperKit-transcribed sessions now carry per-word timing data through to the Transcript Reader, so the active word highlights as audio plays — no more linear interpolation across whole segments. Click anywhere in the transcript to seek to that exact word. The "Word-level sync available" banner stops firing on new local recordings (it only appears now on older sessions that predate this fix).

Diarization on the local engine also benefits: BleedFilter's per-token speaker-assignment path activates, so when two speakers share a Whisper segment, each word goes to whichever speaker the diarizer says is active at that word's start time — replacing the previous winner-take-all overlap voting.

Hebrew language detection: now returns the right default

detectLanguage was returning ("en", 0) on all-Hebrew audio — a meaningless default that runtime had been quietly papering over via the fraction > 0.5 gate. Now correctly returns ("he", 0) on every short-circuit path (model load failure, audio < 10s, no voiced windows above the RMS threshold, any thrown error).

Bundles all of v0.10.5 (Local Me/Them + BleedFilter word-overlap rewrite) and v0.11 (AI Naming sidebar truncation) for users on auto-update jumping straight from v0.10.4.x → v0.12. 445 tests passing.

item.title: Version 0.11 — Local Me/Them + BleedFilter word-overlap + sidebar fix
item.description:
Local Me/Them — WhisperKit-backed, no Soniox required

Me/Them mode (the Me-vs-everyone-else two-bucket transcript) used to require a Soniox API key. v0.11 ships a local Me/Them pipeline that runs natively on WhisperKit — pick Local + Me / Them in the re-process popover and the worker dispatches to the new dual-stream local pipeline.

  • Two sequential WhisperKit transcribes on mic + system, plus per-stream language detection with a 10s duration gate (a noisy short mic stream can't override the meeting language).
  • Heartbeat status during transcribe — "Transcribing microphone (1:30)…" ticks every 15s instead of a frozen string.
  • Calendar attendees drive the "Me + N others" header; degrades cleanly to "Me + others" when no calendar match.
  • Watchdog raised to 6× audio duration for the local Me/Them path (was 3× for full-diar local).

BleedFilter: word-overlap replaces temporal overlap

The previous bleed detection (Detection B) discarded mic activity with >50% temporal overlap with system speech, regardless of text. On Whisper's coarse 10–30s segments, that meant any user interjection during continuous remote speech got dropped as "bleed" — even when the user said something completely different.

The new rule asks the semantically correct question: did the mic capture the same words the system was playing? — by computing a lowercased token-set overlap. True echo (high word overlap) still discards. Distinct parallel speech now survives.

  • Real-audio validation: on a 13-minute Hebrew call, ~26% of "Me" segments survive that would have been silently discarded under the old rule.
  • Affects both backends — Soniox path got the same fix incidentally. The blindspot existed before but was usually masked by Soniox's >0.8s silence-gap cut rule.
  • Detection A (text-equality within ±5s) is unchanged — true echo still caught.

Settings sidebar — AI Naming label fully fixed

Three commits, one chain:

  • Non-breaking space glues "AI Naming" to the ampersand so "&" doesn't widow on its own line.
  • Top-aligned icon + badge with the label capped at 2 lines.
  • Final fix: dropped the Spacer() between the label and the badge in favor of .frame(maxWidth: .infinity, alignment: .leading) directly on the Text. The previous layout left only ~83pt for the label, which the medium-weight selected state edged past — truncating "Summaries" to "Summaries…". The new layout gives ~91pt, enough for "AI Naming &" on line 1 with "Summaries" alone on line 2 in either selected or deselected state.

item.title: Version 0.10.5 — Local Me/Them + BleedFilter word-overlap fix
item.description:
Local Me/Them — WhisperKit-backed, no Soniox required

Me/Them mode (the Me-vs-everyone-else two-bucket transcript) used to require a Soniox API key. v0.10.5 ships a local Me/Them pipeline that runs natively on WhisperKit — pick Local + Me / Them in the re-process popover and the worker dispatches to the new dual-stream local pipeline.

  • Two sequential WhisperKit transcribes on mic + system, plus per-stream language detection with a 10s duration gate (a noisy short mic stream can't override the meeting language).
  • Heartbeat status during transcribe — "Transcribing microphone (1:30)…" ticks every 15s instead of a frozen string.
  • Calendar attendees drive the "Me + N others" header; degrades cleanly to "Me + others" when no calendar match.
  • Watchdog raised to 6× audio duration for the local Me/Them path (was 3× for full-diar local).

BleedFilter: word-overlap replaces temporal overlap

The previous bleed detection (Detection B) discarded mic activity with >50% temporal overlap with system speech, regardless of text. On Whisper's coarse 10–30s segments, that meant any user interjection during continuous remote speech got dropped as "bleed" — even when the user said something completely different.

The new rule asks the semantically correct question: did the mic capture the same words the system was playing? — by computing a lowercased token-set overlap. True echo (high word overlap) still discards. Distinct parallel speech now survives.

  • Real-audio validation: on a 13-minute Hebrew call, ~26% of "Me" segments survive that would have been silently discarded under the old rule.
  • Affects both backends — Soniox path got the same fix incidentally. The blindspot existed before but was usually masked by Soniox's >0.8s silence-gap cut rule.
  • Detection A (text-equality within ±5s) is unchanged — true echo still caught.

Other fixes

  • Settings sidebar — "AI Naming & Summaries" no longer wraps to a 3-line widow; ampersand glued to "Naming" via non-breaking space.

item.title: Version 0.10.4.2 — Settings polish + language-detection default-on
item.description:
Settings UI consistency + language-detection default-on

v0.10.4 introduced the chrome system but left four .pickerStyle(.segmented) controls inside Settings still painting in system blue. This patch migrates all four to the canonical KeraSegmented component (matching the Library and Speakers Library filter pills): Engine (Local / Soniox), Speaker separation (Local / Cloud), AI Naming Provider (OpenAI / Anthropic), Theme (System / Light / Dark).

SwiftUI .tint(Color.kerInk) propagates from the Settings root, flipping every Toggle's on-state from system blue to ink. Belt-and-suspenders alongside the explicit segmented migrations.

Language detection now defaults to ON for new installs. Previously read via UserDefaults.standard.bool(forKey:) which conflates "off" with "unset" — Hebrew transcripts on a default-English model were noticeably worse. Existing users who explicitly turned it off keep that choice.

item.title: Version 0.10.4.1 — Transcript window hotfix
item.description:
Hotfix: transcript window opens at the right size

v0.10.4 shipped with a regression: opening any non-trivial transcript produced a window much taller than the screen, hiding the resize handle. The clamp from the prior fix wasn't actually doing anything — NSHostingView's default sizingOptions propagated SwiftUI's full preferred height up to the window after the explicit setContentSize call had already run.

Two-line fix: clear sizingOptions on the hosting view so SwiftUI's preferred size never propagates back; set contentMaxSize as an upper auto-resize bound the user can still drag past via the resize handle.

Verified: a transcript that previously reported 880×3725 on a 1512×982 display now opens at exactly 880×720.

item.title: Version 0.10.4 — Window chrome system
item.description:
Kera Identity v3 — window chrome system

Every Kera window now reads as a single document: paper ground everywhere, centered floating titles, hairline structure, 8% ink wash for selection. Light and dark both supported.

  • All 6 windows unified — Settings, Activity Log, Permissions Setup, Library, Speakers Library, Speaker Editor, Transcript Reader. Transparent titlebar, paper bleeds up, centered title floats on the new chrome band.
  • "Library" — Meeting Library renamed to "Library" per the design handoff. The "Import audio" button now lives in the SwiftUI title row instead of a separate AppKit titlebar accessory.
  • Filter chips, unified — Library and Speakers Library filter pills now share the same KeraSegmented control. Selected pills paint with kerInk; counts visible with monospaced digits.
  • Selection wash — list-row selection uses kerInk @ 8% across Settings, Speakers Library, and Speaker Editor.
  • Hairlines, not borders — every structural rule normalized to 0.5pt kerHair.

Other fixes

  • "Auto-name with AI" → "AI Naming & Summaries" — toggle label now reflects that the setting also gates v0.10.3's library AI summaries, not just auto-naming.
  • Transcript window sizing — clamped initial frame to screen so transcripts no longer open larger than the display; cascade offset preserved.

Plan rivaled across 20 rounds (converged at 2 consecutive clean rounds); code rivaled across 1 round, catching 4 layout/contract bugs the plan-level review hadn't seen.
